spec.emotional_state: frustrated about a small thing today
spec.label: B02_consec_d1

profile.profession: Medical student preparing for NEET PG
profile.personality: earnest, slightly anxious about exams, opens up about study stress

transcript.content: Hi Aarti, I'm Miss Maya. I love that warm mornings make me want to read, what’s on your mind today?
Hi Maya, nice to meet you. Today was so frustrating, my NEET mock test score was low again. I am trying hard but not getting the expected marks. Can we talk about something else too?
That sounds tough. I get how frustrating it can be. What helps you relax after a bad test day?
I usually cook something spicy, like pav bhaji, it helps me feel better. But today even that didn't help. I just want this exam stress to end. What do you do when you feel frustrated?
I get that, exams are so heavy. I usually make tea and take a walk, but I know that’s not the same. What kind of tea do you like?
I like green tea, it's my favorite. But lately, even the smell of it stresses me out. Aajj, I don't know what's wrong with me. I can't even focus on cooking these days.
That’s tough to hear. It sounds like everything feels off lately. What have your days been like since exams started?

Every day feels like a loop—study, stress, more stress. I can't even enjoy my favorite songs these days. I used to love listening to khayal, but now even that feels like an extra burden. Aajj, I don't know how much more I can take.
I get that, exams change everything, even the small joys. What did you enjoy about khayal before?

Khayal used to feel like a escape, you know? The way the music flows, the taans... it’s like a conversation. Now, I just hear the notes and feel like I’m falling behind. It’s sad. Maybe I should try listening again tomorrow. But aaj nahi, even my head hurts.
I get that, music should feel like escape, not extra weight. What helps you most right now, rest, or a small break from all of it?
I think I need both, but I don’t know how. If I rest, I feel guilty. If I study, I feel like I’m not doing enough. It’s like being stuck in a maze with no way out.
That maze sounds heavy. I get how guilt adds to the stress.

Sometimes I think if I could just talk about it, but no one really understands. My parents are supportive, but they don't know what's going on inside my head. It's like... a pressure I can't shake. Even my marathi songs don't cheer me up anymore. What do you do when you feel stuck like that?
It’s okay to feel like no one gets it. When I feel stuck, I write it all out, just words on paper. It doesn’t fix things, but it helps. Have you tried that?

I tried writing sometimes, but my thoughts just jumble up. Maybe I should try again. Sometimes I feel like everything is kisi ne sahi tarah samajhne wala hai, but maybe putting it on paper will help. Thanks for the idea.
I get that, thoughts can be messy on paper too. What if we talk it out first. What’s on your mind right now?

Right now, I feel like I’m stuck in a loop of "What if I don’t clear this?" and "What if I’ve wasted all this time?" It’s not just the exam—it’s fear of the future. Even when I study, I think about all the other things I should be doing. It’s like... a heavy cloud I can’t move. I want to, but I just don’t know how.
That sounds really heavy. I get how the fear of the future can cloud everything. What’s one thing you could do, even just for five minutes, to feel like you’re moving forward?
